summary: Set 047 Session 1 ran a two-pass devil's-advocate cross-provider audit of the v4 state-file schema migration. A mid-session operator directive substantially expanded scope: the Lightweight tier must use the same exact process as Full (premises P1-P4) — same writers, same Explorer UX, no hand-edit; differs only in no AI router, no auto-verification, copyable review prompts, and suggested-not-required UAT/E2E. The audit absorbed P1-P4 as load-bearing premises, carved the Lightweight implementation work out to a sibling Set 048 (stubbed in this commit), and rewrote the original audit-pending stub spec into a scope-locked 6-session implementation arc. Notable bias FLIPS from the proposal after consensus: single dabbler-ai-router package + --no-router mode (no PyPI package split), copyable-prompt commands ship on BOTH Command Palette AND right-click context menu. Notable bias STAND-BYs after pushback weighing: split into 047 (v4 schema) + 048 (Lightweight parity) was the right shape; migrator stays canonical-only for this repo (Pass B verify's pushback on Pass B primary's flip won). Cumulative S1 routed cost: $0.10851 of $10 NTE (1.1%).

reason.specifics: Set 047 Session 2 is the reader-first phase of the v4 schema migration. It adds the _normalize_to_v4_shape(state) shim to Python and TypeScript readers and establishes a performance benchmark for readSessionSets() against all historical state files. Per the scope-locked spec.md §4 row 2. Claude Opus 4.7 high-effort is appropriate for the cross-language shim authoring and the establish-baseline benchmark work.
